title: Rich Midnight Peruvian Cheese Tagine
description: A chilled butter, cheese-pull Peruvian dish featuring a citrusy flavor profile, best served in a stone bowl ceramic tagine.
instructions: Heat butter in a ceramic tagine at 375�00F for 10 minutes to melt and create a rich aroma.
Add sliced Gruyere cheese, Greek yogurt, and Queso fresco. Stir until combined and slightly melted.
Mix in citrus-herb seasoning, black pepper, lemon juice, salt, and chopped onion.
Add sliced red and yellow corn and stir until coated with the cheese mixture.
Pour in water to cover the mixture, then add aji amarillo pepper slices and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to 325�00F and let cook for 20 minutes with the cover on, occasionally stirring and scraping the tagine.
Stir in chopped cilantro just before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with extra cilantro and Gruyere cheese shavings, if desired.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the cheese to set and create a beautiful pull.
Reheat the tagine in the oven or on the stovetop before serving, if needed.
